description: ResMed is a health technology company selling connected sleep apnea and respiratory care devices, masks, and care-management software around a proprietary home-care cloud ecosystem.

overview.title: Business Footprint
paragraphs: ResMed sits at the intersection of medical devices, recurring consumables, and healthcare workflow software. Its core franchise is sleep and breathing health: CPAP/APAP machines, bilevel and ventilation devices, masks, accessories, and the remote-monitoring infrastructure that follows patients home from the clinic.

The company also owns residential-care software assets for durable medical equipment providers, pharmacies, home health, hospice, senior living, and other post-acute settings. In plain English, it sells the machine by the bed, the mask on the face, and a good portion of the paperwork orbiting both.

overview.title: Product Surface
paragraphs: AirSense 11 is the anchor consumer-facing device in the seed context. It combines CPAP/APAP therapy modes with myAir onboarding, coaching, Care Check-In, Bluetooth/app pairing, and over-the-air updates - useful therapy features, and also a reminder that even airflow now ships with account flows.

AirFit is a broad mask family rather than a single product, spanning nasal, nasal-pillow, full-face, and newer tube-up designs. The masks are clinically boring in the best sense - fit, seal, comfort, replacement schedules - but they are central to the recurring supplies business.

overview.title: Software And Data Layer
paragraphs: AirView gives clinicians and providers remote patient monitoring, compliance reporting, remote troubleshooting, and care coordination for sleep and ventilation therapy. myAir provides patient coaching and daily feedback, while ReSupply and related tools connect adherence, replacement schedules, and DME operations.

The residential-care software side expands the registry target beyond bedside hardware. Brightree, MatrixCare, CitusHealth, Somnoware, and MEDIFOX DAN make ResMed relevant to EHR, billing, care collaboration, patient engagement, and post-acute workflow comparisons.

overview.title: Registry Angle
paragraphs: For Free The World, the most useful comparison buckets are connected CPAP/APAP devices, mask consumables, patient coaching apps, remote monitoring portals, DME/HME business software, and post-acute EHR/care platforms. Open alternatives are likely to be uneven: hardware is regulated, masks are physical and fitted, and software lock-in is where the usual proprietary gravity does its quiet work.

A current sync should also check recent portfolio movement such as the 2026 Noctrix Health acquisition, because it may add a non-pharmacologic sleep-therapy device line that does not fit neatly under the existing AirSense/AirFit seed list.
